highlights: One truncation hazard: child-agents.jsonl initialized with 'w' (bin/stampede.sh:853).
Atomic rename (.tmp + replace) consistent for non-JSONL artifacts.
All appended records end with explicit '\n'; sizes well under PIPE_BUF.
Readers tolerate missing files; partial-line tolerance is implicit (wc -l).

evidence.note: atomic_append_guidance: append-only-jsonl, never rewrite/truncate, tolerate malformed trailing lines.
HAZARD: child-agents.jsonl opened with 'w' for first record.
logs use 'a'; result writes use .tmp-{task_id}.json + os.rename.

gaps: Append-only is contract-only; no runtime enforcement against rogue 'w' opens.
Readers don't JSON-parse; malformed lines silently inflate counts.
swarm-state.json uses full overwrite via atomic rename; protocol does not explicitly carve this out.
No flock; relies solely on O_APPEND atomicity for short records.
recommendations: Change bin/stampede.sh:853 from 'w' to 'a' for child-agents.jsonl init.
Document in protocol.json that swarm-state.json uses atomic-replace, not append.
Add monitor canary that flags ledger size decreases (truncation).
Document PIPE_BUF constraint to guard future record growth.
